title: Cool Crunchy Ethiopian Perilla Leaf Flatbread
description: A fermented, energizing Ethiopian dish featuring perilla leaf, double rise bread, and a glassy jelly texture, all dairy free friendly. This unique flatbread is perfect for a snack or light meal.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the warm water and yeast, stirring to dissolve the yeast, then let it sit for 5 minutes.
Add the flour, salt, sugar, and non dairy yogurt to the bowl, mixing until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Punch down the dough and roll it out into a large circle, about 1/4 inch thick.
Brush the dough with olive oil and sprinkle with perilla leaves.
Let the flatbread rise for another 30 minutes, then bake it in a preheated oven at 400 degrees for 20 minutes, or until it is golden brown.
While the flatbread is baking, combine the agar agar powder, lemon juice, and 1/4 cup of water in a small saucepan, whisking to dissolve the agar agar.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the mixture has thickened into a glassy jelly.
Let the flatbread cool for 5 minutes, then top it with the glassy jelly and serve.
